vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely inconsistent with the visual evidence. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent or dormant. P2 (Mexican Mint) is absent. P3 (Pothos) is present but in a state of severe decline. P4 (Silver Guest) is absent.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss (Yellow pot appears empty/soil only)
inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss (Black pot appears empty/soil only)
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Present (Pothos specimen with white rabbit anchor)
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss (No evidence of shared occupant in P2)
inventory_reconciliation.New_Introductions: None. The presence of eggshell fragments in P3 is an anthropogenic intervention.

plant_audit.P1_Yellow_Pot: Empty. Soil surface shows no signs of active growth.
plant_audit.P3_Pothos: Single leaf specimen. Shows progressive chlorosis and loss of turgor pressure over the 5-day sequence.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Appears consistently damp/dark across all pots.
biome_observations.debris: Eggshell fragments observed in P3. Electronic component (IC chip) observed on desk surface in early images, removed in later images.

temporal_deltas.T_minus_5_to_T_minus_1: P3 Pothos leaf shows gradual drooping and loss of structural integrity. The electronic component on the desk was removed.
temporal_deltas.Current: Total loss of visibility due to lighting failure or extreme low-light conditions. P3 is barely discernible.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The Pothos (P3) is in critical condition, likely suffering from root rot or severe environmental stress (lack of light/improper moisture). The other pots are effectively barren, indicating a total failure of the expected biome.
vision_report.anomalies: The presence of eggshells suggests an attempt at soil amendment, which has not yielded positive growth results. The removal of the electronic component suggests human interaction with the desk space.
vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is in a state of collapse. The only remaining specimen, a single Pothos leaf in P3, is failing. The yellow pot (P1) and the secondary black pot (P2) are devoid of the registered flora. The environment is currently too dark to perform a high-fidelity assessment, suggesting a failure in the fixed lighting system or a transition to a nocturnal state that precludes observation.
